subject: [PATCH 6/8 RFC] mm/memcontrol: optimize memsw stock for cgroup v1

2026-04-10.patch_summary: This patch optimizes memory usage tracking in cgroup v1 by introducing a new stock for the memsw page counter, which is charged and uncharged independently from the memory page counter. This allows for better caching and reduces the worst-case scenario of having to walk two page_counter hierarchies. The change provides three possible scenarios: best case (both counters use cached stock), halfway scenario (one counter uses cached stock while the other doesn't), and worst case (neither counter uses cached stock). By introducing an independent stock for memsw, the patch aims to avoid the worst-case scenario more often and allow for separate failure or success of memory page counter and memsw page counter.
